reasoning: The forecast concerns whether NextEra Energy's (NEE) stock price will be higher on 2026-01-24 compared to 2025-10-26, representing approximately a 3-month (90-day) holding period. In the absence of specific real-time signals, estimating the direction of a stock over a 3-month period relies heavily on historical base rates of equity returns. Historically, equities exhibit a positive drift. For a utility stock like NEE with an annualized volatility around 20-25% and expected annual price return around 5-6% (accounting for part of the total return being drawn as dividends), the 90-day expected price return is around 1.25% to 1.5%. The corresponding 90-day volatility is approximately 10-12%. Consequently, the probability of observing a positive price return over a 3-month period is the chance of drawing a positive value from a normal distribution with mean 1.25% and standard deviation 10%, which is approximately 54-56%. As a result, 0.55 is a well-calibrated baseline estimate.
